title: Postharvest Food Loss Measurement And Reduction Potential In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping postharvest food loss at sub-national scale in SSA, with explicit loss-estimation methodology documentation, crop-specific loss data, and the distinction between modeled estimates and verified household-level measurement.
why_it_matters: FAO estimates 30-40 percent of food produced in SSA is lost postharvest — enough to feed 300 million people. Hermetic storage can reduce on-farm storage losses by 50-80 percent but adoption is below 10 percent. Cold-chain for perishable crops is almost absent outside urban corridors. Without verified sub-national loss analysis, storage-infrastructure and food-system investment cannot be targeted where losses are highest and most reducible.

success_metrics.target: Every loss estimate documents whether data comes from APHLIS provincial modeling, FAO country aggregates, or household-survey direct measurement, and explicitly states methodology differences.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against loss-estimation methodology documentation.

success_metrics.target: No loss claim is merged without documenting which crops and which postharvest stages (handling, storage, transport, processing) are covered.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms crop-specific and stage-specific loss documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every loss estimate explicitly states whether it is a modeled estimate from APHLIS/FAO or a directly measured household-survey observation.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms modeled-vs-measured distinction in every loss claim.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, loss-estimation safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
